events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: flashing not lapped/sealed in a residential masonry/concrete job, rough-complete stage. This is a defect — flashing must be lapped and sealed per TMS 402 and BIA tech notes. This is non-compliant and installed-defective. Decision should be fail. Risk is high because improper flashing leads to water intrusion.

"findings": [
"Flashing is not lapped or sealed at joints/terminations",
"Missing continuity of flashing will allow water penetration behind masonry veneer",
"Weeps may be ineffective if flashing does not direct water to the drainage cavity"
],
"actions": [
"Re-lap flashing a minimum of 2 inches at end joints and seal per manufacturer instructions",
"Install end dams at terminations and ensure flashing slopes to the exterior",
"Verify weep openings are placed immediately above flashing at correct spacing"
],
"rationale": "Flashing in masonry veneer must be continuous, lapped, sealed, and integrated with weeps to manage water. Unlapped/unsealed flashing is a defective installation and non-compliant with TMS 402 and BIA detailing requirements.",

"BIA Technical Notes on flashing and weep holes in brick veneer"
]
